title: Garlicky Delightful Peruvian Fish Paella
description: A flavorful Peruvian-inspired fish paella with a garlicky twist, crunchy dried mint, and a sticky lacquer glaze.
instructions: Heat the olive oil over medium heat in a large paella pan.
Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped dried mint and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the sea bass pieces to the pan and cook until browned on all sides, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the paella rice to the pan and stir to coat the rice in the oil and mix with the fish and garlic.
Add the warmed chicken broth to the pan, 1 cup at a time, stirring constantly and allowing each cup to be absorbed before adding the next.
Once the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed, add the red pepper flakes and stir to combine.
Transfer the paella pan to the oven and bake for 5 minutes to crisp the rice.
Remove the paella from the oven and top with the lacquer glaze and queso fresco crumbles.
Return the paella to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the paella from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
